description: Outback pub and accommodation in historic Pine Creek built from mud brick (termite mounds) and local timber. Relaxed Top End atmosphere with restaurant and birdwatching at Kakadu's doorstep.

name: Mataranka Homestead
description: Iconic outback resort next to thermal pools set in lush tropical rainforest maintaining 34 degrees year-round. Cabins, motel rooms, and camping with genuine outback hospitality.

name: Territory Manor Motel
description: Unique rammed-earth motel accommodation in Mataranka, 100km south of Katherine. Twenty-six rooms with outdoor pool, licensed restaurant, and walking distance to Bitter Springs thermal pools.

name: Bullo River Station
description: 400,000-acre working cattle property and luxury lodge in the Victoria River region near the WA border. Twelve guest rooms plus a remote hilltop hut accessible only by helicopter. Conservation at its core.

name: Lorella Springs Wilderness Park
description: One-million-acre privately run station in the Gulf of Carpentaria with accommodation and camping amid dramatic landscapes. Remote wilderness with thermal springs and waterfalls.

name: Longitude 131
description: Sixteen luxury tented pavilions perched on red sand dunes with uninterrupted Uluru views from every bed. Owned by Baillie Lodges, combining sustainable design with gourmet dining and private touring.

name: Curtin Springs Wayside Inn
description: Multi-generational family cattle station and wayside inn one hour from Uluru. Ensuite rooms, budget rooms, and camping offering a genuine Central Australian pastoral experience.

name: Kings Creek Station
description: Working cattle station 36km from Kings Canyon offering luxury glamping in the Dreamtime Escarpment and Drovers Dream tents with king beds and air conditioning, set among majestic desert oaks.

name: Wilmots on Dixon
description: Family-owned B&B in Alice Springs run by hosts Ian and Helen since 1996. Private rooms with garden or pool views, complimentary bikes, outdoor pool, and highly praised buffet breakfast.

name: Squeakywindmill Boutique Tent B&B
description: Luxury glamping tent B&B outside Alice Springs with mountain views, private terrace, fully equipped kitchenette, walk-in shower, and bathrobes. A unique outback experience.

name: Over the Hump
description: Unique self-contained stable accommodation in Alice Springs where guests enjoy visits from local kangaroos, goannas, and dingoes. A truly distinctive outback stay.

name: Desert Palms Alice Springs
description: Eighty individual studio villas set around a free-form pool with waterfall, surrounded by tropical gardens on the banks of the Todd River. Each villa has ensuite, kitchenette, and verandah.

name: Alice on Todd Apartments
description: Fifty-seven self-contained apartments overlooking the Todd River, from studios to two-bedroom deluxe. Independent property with outdoor pool and home-away-from-home feel.

name: Desert Rose Inn
description: Budget-friendly independent motel in the heart of Alice Springs with saltwater pool, communal kitchen, BBQ area, and guest lounge. Practical outback base near Alice Springs Train Station.

name: Elkira Court Motel
description: Independent motel in downtown Alice Springs at the Todd Mall with 58 rooms, outdoor pool, barbecue facilities, and on-site restaurant. Central location near Aboriginal art galleries.

name: Annie's Place
description: Independent guesthouse on Traeger Avenue in The Gap, about 1.2km from Alice Springs centre. Affordable and characterful accommodation in the Red Centre.

name: Heavitree Gap Outback Lodge
description: Independent lodge at the southern entrance to Alice Springs nestled at the base of the MacDonnell Ranges. Hand-feed wild black-footed rock wallabies at sunset. Eighty rooms with balconies and caravan sites.

name: Safari Lodge Motel
description: Award-winning, proudly 100% Aboriginal-owned motel in the heart of Tennant Creek. A genuine outback stay supporting Indigenous enterprise on the Stuart Highway.

name: Bluestone Motor Inn
description: Independent motor inn in Tennant Creek with outdoor pool and restaurant. A comfortable and practical stop on the Stuart Highway between Alice Springs and Darwin.

name: Goldfields Hotel Motel
description: Independent 3-star venue in Tennant Creek with 24 air-conditioned rooms, two pubs, and a dining room. Classic outback hotel on the Stuart Highway.

name: Tilmouth Well Roadhouse
description: Remote outback roadhouse on the iconic Tanami Road, 200km northwest of Alice Springs. Air-conditioned rooms, restaurant and bar, pool, and sprawling lawns under vast outback skies.

name: Batchelor Holiday Park
description: Independent holiday park at the gateway to Litchfield National Park, 98km south of Darwin. Family cabins, luxury two-bedroom villas, and a range of caravan and camping sites.
